km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: William Turner於1450年出生在英國。他於1470年與Ann在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Turner于1450年出生在英国。他于1470年与Ann在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Turner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English t(o)urnour, turner ‘turner’ (Old French to(u)rn(e)our), mainly denoting someone who fashioned small objects of wood, metal, or bone on a lathe, but also a variety of other occupations, including turnspit and translator or interpreter. This surname may have become confused with Toner . In North America, it is also very common among African Americans.

English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Middle English turn-hare, a compound of Middle English tournen ‘to turn, direct, steer’ + hare ‘hare’, a name for someone in charge of the greyhounds in hare coursing or an exaggerated compliment for someone who could run fast. See also Turnbull .
English: perhaps also from Middle English t(o)urn(e)our ‘jouster, one who takes part in a tournament’ (Old French tornoieor, tournoieur).

variants.name: Trinder
meanings: English (Oxfordshire and Gloucestershire): occupational name from a derivative of Middle English trenden ‘to turn, roll, twist’ (Old English trendan), denoting someone who turned something such as wood or fibres. Compare Turner , Spinner .

variants.name: Toner
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tomhrair ‘descendant of Tomhrar’, a Gaelicized form of the Norse personal name Thorr. Compare Thunder .
English: nickname from Old French toner ‘thunder’, perhaps for someone with a thunderous voice. Compare Thunder .

variants.name: Tarner
meanings: English: variant of   Turner  . This form of the surname is relatively rare in Britain and Ireland. It may sometimes have been confused with   Taverner  .
German (North Rhine-Westphalia): unexplained. This surname is rare in Germany.

variants.name: Turney
meanings: habitational name from any of the places in northern France called Tournai-sur-Dive (Orne), Tournay-sur-Odon (Calvados), or Tourny (Eure), or occasionally from Tournai in Hainaut (Belgium).

perhaps occasionally an occupational name from Middle English tournei, turnai ‘lawyer’ (Anglo-Norman French tourné), or a nickname from Middle English tournei(en) ‘(to) joust, take part in a tournament’ (Old French torneier). Compare Turner .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: William Turner was born in 1450, in England. He married Ann in 1470, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: William Turner est né en 1450, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Ann en 1470, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: William Turner wurde 1450 in England geboren. Er hat 1470 in England Ann geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: William Turner è nato il nel 1450 nel England. Lui sposato Ann nel 1470, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: William Turnerは1450年にイギリスで生まれた。1470年にイギリスでAnnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: William Turner(은)는 1450년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1470년 영국에서 Ann(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Turner nasceu em 1450, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Ann em 1470, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: William Turner родился в 1450 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann в 1470 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: William Turner nació en 1450, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Ann en 1470, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
